data.title: Scale worktree isolation to parallel review with Clash conflict prevention
data.description:
## Context

Epic A (dso-to7x) delivers worktree isolation with serial review — sub-agents implement in isolated worktrees, orchestrator reviews/tests each worktree one at a time, commits and merges sequentially. This works but leaves review/test throughput as a bottleneck: with N worktrees, review time is N × single-review-time.

Epic B scales review to parallel: all returned worktrees are reviewed and tested simultaneously, with Clash preventing file conflicts proactively during implementation.

## Success Criteria

1. Clash (clash-sh/clash) integrates at the orchestrator level via file-impact pre-analysis before dispatch, preventing two sub-agents from being assigned overlapping files — verified by test showing dispatch blocked when file lists overlap
2. When implementation sub-agents return, the orchestrator dispatches review and test sub-agents in parallel — one pair per worktree — verified by concurrent review producing separate reviewer-findings.json files in each worktrees ARTIFACTS_DIR
3. First-pass-first-merge by completion order: each worktree merges as soon as its review passes, without waiting for other worktrees — verified by merge order matching completion order, not dispatch order
4. Review timeout per worktree: hung reviews are skipped after configurable timeout and flagged for user attention — verified by injecting a slow review and asserting it is skipped after timeout
5. Hook dispatcher updated to route Clash pre-analysis through the consolidated hook architecture

## Dependencies

- Depends on dso-to7x (Epic A: worktree isolation with serial review)
- Coordinate with dso-t7af (usage-aware throttling: controls how many parallel agents can dispatch)
